mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in industrial production indicates expanding real economic activity, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO growth and 60-day forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the signal produces negative returns more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in the unemployment rate over three months signals improving labor market conditions, which lifts consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines > 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: When the trigger fires, 20-day forward returns are negative more than 45% of the time, or the average return is not significantly positive at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is near zero and unemployment remains elevated, the Federal Reserve is likely to maintain accommodative policy, compressing discount rates and supporting equity valuations, producing positive forward returns.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS < 1% and UNRATE > 6%, the forward 20-day S&P 500 return is negative more than 50% of the time or the cumulative return over all such periods is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts in a low interest rate environment signal strengthening residential investment and consumer confidence, which supports economic growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 2%, an increase in HOUST is followed by negative 60-day forward returns more than 40% of the time, or the correlation is not positive at the 10% significance level.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in consumer price inflation, proxied by a monthly CPI index increase exceeding 0.5%, raises expectations of monetary tightening, which contracts valuation multiples and depresses S&P 500 returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases by more than 0.5% month-over-month
failure_condition: When CPI spikes occur, the average 5-day forward return is positive, or the negative return rate is below 60%.

mechanism_narrative: Robust growth in real retail sales indicates strong consumer demand, which drives corporate earnings and supports higher equity prices over the next month.
failure_condition: The Spearman rank correlation between monthly RSAFS growth and subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns is less than 0.05 over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent positive growth in both real GDP and nonfarm payrolls confirms a broad-based expansion in output and employment, which boosts earnings visibility and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: both GDPC1 and PAYEMS show positive change over their respective prior periods
failure_condition: When both proxies are rising simultaneously, the 60-day forward S&P 500 return is negative more than 45% of the time, or the average return is not significantly positive at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.5%, monetary policy is highly accommodative, reducing the cost of capital and encouraging risk-taking, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, the 20-day forward S&P 500 return is negative more than 50% of the time, or the average return is not positive at the 10% significance level.
